---
title: "Appendix: Data Sources and Accessions"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    toc: true
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Sequence Data

| Source | Description | Access | Citation |
|---|---|---|---|
| ICTV OPSR alignment | Potyviridae polyprotein/CI alignment (v16) | `data/OPSR.Poty.Fig3.v16_align.txt` | [@inouenagata2022] |
| ICTV VMR (MSL41) | Virus metadata / genus assignment | `data/VMR_MSL41.v1.20260320.xlsx` | [@ictvvmr2024] |
| NCBI GenBank | CDS sequences retrieved via `rentrez` | `data/ci_cds_fetched.fasta` | — |
| ELM database | Short linear motif definitions | API / `gget elm`; `data/elm_gget/` | [@elm2024] |

## External Databases

| Database | Usage | API endpoint |
|---|---|---|
| GBIF | Taxonomic resolution, occurrence data | `api.gbif.org/v1/` |
| GLOBI | Biotic interaction data | `api.globalbioticinteractions.org/` |
| ELM | Protein short linear motifs | `elm.eu.org/api/` |
| NCBI Entrez | Nucleotide/protein sequences | `eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/` |

## Software and R packages

All analyses were carried out in **R** [@R-base] with **Quarto**. The work relies
heavily on the **rOpenSci** ecosystem — `rentrez` for NCBI Entrez access
[@R-rentrez], `rotl` for the Open Tree of Life [@R-rotl], `rphylopic` for
silhouettes [@R-rphylopic], and `crul` for HTTP [@R-crul] — alongside the
Bioconductor sequence/tree stack (`Biostrings` [@R-Biostrings], `DECIPHER`
[@R-DECIPHER], `msa` [@R-msa], `ggtree`/`treeio` [@R-ggtree; @R-treeio]), the
phylogenetics packages `ape` [@R-ape] and `phytools` [@R-phytools], codon/sequence
tools `seqinr` [@R-seqinr] and `ggseqlogo` [@R-ggseqlogo], the network packages
`igraph` [@R-igraph], `ggraph` [@R-ggraph] and `visNetwork` [@R-visNetwork],
interactive widgets `plotly` [@R-plotly], `DT` [@R-DT] and `r3dmol` [@R-r3dmol],
`metacoder` [@R-metacoder], and the `tidyverse` [@R-tidyverse]. Analysis functions
specific to this project are collected in the local **`potytools`** package
[@R-potytools]. **Every package used, with its version, is listed in the session
information below, and all software citations are collected in the complete
bibliography.**

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: false
# Session info with absolute filesystem paths redacted (privacy).
.si <- capture.output(sessioninfo::session_info())
.si <- gsub("[A-Za-z]:[\\\\/][^ ]*|/(home|Users)/[^ ]*", "<path redacted>", .si)
cat(.si, sep = "\n")
```

## Reproducibility

All analyses can be re-run end-to-end with:

```r
targets::tar_make()
```

See `_targets.R` for the full dependency graph. Individual steps requiring
external tools (`mafft`, `iqtree2`, `hmmscan`) are noted where they appear.
